title: E8 media production
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2kedf","text":"This protocol describes the procedure to prepare E8 Supplement and complete E8 medium for hPSC maintenance culture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5srpt","text":"We recommend storage of all the media components in their recommended storage conditions up to one year. Similarly, we recommend that the prepared E8 supplement be stored in -80oC up to one year. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":177,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"a9qkk","text":"Sodium selenite is considered a hazardous substance. It is toxic and can be harmful if ingested, inhaled, or absorbed through the skin. It is also classified as an environmental hazard, particularly harmful to aquatic life.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2bpr1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2v3tu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: %s %s Storage conditions should follow manufacturer instructions. For FGF2 and TGFß1 long term storage is recommended at -80°C.
L-Ascorbic acid 2-phosphate
Assembly of E8 Supplement
Preparation of supplemented E8 media (0.5 L)
Insulin, Transferrin and Sodium Selenite
Sodium Selenite stock solution 700 µg/mL
Human recombinant FGF2 Stock 200 µg/mL
Human recombinant TGFß1 Stock 100 µg/mL
Weight 3.2 g of L-ascorbic acid 2-phosphate using analytical scale.
Add 50 mL of distilled water in a 100 mL clean glass beaker.
Slowly add prepared L-ascorbic acid 2-phosphate to the water in the glass beaker.
Stir solution until it is clear. Do not store this solution. Prepare it fresh.
Dilute 70 mg of Sodium Selenite in 100 mL distilled water to prepare a 700 µg/mL solution
Prepare 1 mL aliquots in 1.5 mL tubes
Store at -20 °C until usage
Add 45 mL of distilled water to a 100 mL clean glass beaker containing a stirring bar.
Place the beaker on a stirring plate and apply low speed.
Add 1 g of Insulin, the solution becomes white.
To dissolve Insulin adjust to pH 3 adding drop-wise HCl 1 Molarity (M) .
When solution gets clear, adjust pH 7.4 adding drop-wise NaOH 10 Molarity (M) .

Keep stirring the solution the whole time. Expect a starting pH 4.5 and use about 13 drops HCL. Adjusting back pH 7.4 will need about 3 drops NaOH. At ~ pH 6 the solutions gets white again but clears up immediately after.
While stirring Insulin solution, add 535 mg of Transferrin (expect the powder to be orange)
Add 1 mL of Sodium Selenite stock solution prepared in step 3
Use a 50 mL serological pipette to measure the solution volume and adjust to 50 mL by adding distilled water. Do not store Insulin, Transferrin and Sodium Selenite solution long term. Prepare fresh.
Add 24 mL of cold DPBS (-Mg 2 /-Ca 2 ) to a 50 mL tube
Re-suspend the content of 5 vials of 1 mg vials of hrFGF2
Transfer all to 50 mL tube rinsing original vials
Mix the solution by pipetting up and down using 10 mL pipette Keep recombinant proteins always on ice upon preparation. If needed, stock solution can be aliquoted and stored at -80°C.
Add 1 mL of cold distilled water to 100 µg vial of hrTGFß1
Mix the solution by pipetting up and down using microtiter 1000µl pipette Keep recombinant proteins always on ice upon preparation. If needed, stock solution can be aliquoted and stored at -80°C.
Mix all solutions prepared in steps 2, 4, 5 and 6 in a 250 mL clean bottle
Filter sterile using filter bottle with 0.22 µm filter
Prepare aliquots according to the table below: %s %s Store E8 supplements aliquots at -80 °C until usage. If available use low binding protein tubes for E8 supplement aliquoting and storage.
Thaw one aliquot 1.25 mL of E8 supplement prepared in step 7.
Add 1.25 mL E8 supplement to 0.5 L of DMEM/F12, HEPES.
Add 3.6 mL of 7.5 % volume sodium Bicarbonate solution.
Mix thoroughly all ingredients for a homogeneous composition. Store supplemented E8 media at 4 °C for up to 2 weeks.

Perform hPSC maintenance for at least 3 passages using 2 hPSC lines for which culture behaviour is well characterized in-house. Compare side by side validated E8 media batch with newly prepared one. Refer to protocol: Maintenance of hPSC .

Monitor the culture thorough the process and register hPSC morphology taking images for documentation of batch validation. Refer to protocol Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions for typical morphologies in different media/matrix conditions.
creator: Harald Stachelscheid
